summary: This notebook implements an inference and local evaluation pipeline for a 3D cryo-electron tomography object detection task. It loads a pre-trained model checkpoint, processes large 3D volumes using a memory-efficient sliding-window approach with overlapping slices, and averages the output probability maps to ensure spatial consistency. Post-processing applies class-specific thresholds and connected-component analysis to extract precise 3D coordinates, which are formatted into a competition submission CSV. The notebook also includes a custom evaluation script that matches predictions to ground truth using the Hungarian algorithm and computes a weighted F-beta4 score to simulate the official leaderboard. Readers should learn how to handle large-scale 3D inference, implement robust centroid extraction from probability maps, and replicate competition metrics locally.
competition_flow: Loads pre-trained weights, processes 3D cryo-ET volumes via a sliding-window inference pipeline, extracts 3D particle coordinates using connected components, and generates a CSV submission alongside a custom local evaluation script.
data_reading: Uses custom helper functions (read_one_data, read_one_truth) to load 3D volume tensors and ground truth coordinates from Kaggle input directories.
data_processing: Pads volumes to 640x640, splits 3D volumes into overlapping depth slices (size 64, stride 32), runs inference per slice, averages overlapping probability maps, applies class-specific thresholds (0.05), and extracts centroids from connected components scaled by voxel size (10.012444).
insights: Sliding-window inference with overlapping slices and probability averaging effectively handles large 3D volumes while maintaining spatial consistency.
Connected-component analysis on thresholded probability maps is a robust and efficient way to extract discrete 3D object locations.
Custom evaluation metrics using the Hungarian algorithm for matching and class-weighted F-beta scores can accurately simulate competition leaderboards locally.
